items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. The mock-heroic register of “Vague doubts haunt me once again...” is preserved, and the clipped parallelism “Shpak’s…, the ambassador’s …” keeps the comedic detective-like linkage. No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. The repetitive, banal Soviet-style politeness is retained, and the humor of over-introducing himself as “the Tsar” survives intact. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. The abrupt authoritarian demand is preserved and lands as the intended comic power-trip. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. The archaic/high-register melancholy is preserved, and the insult “old woman” keeps the era-clash humor. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Keeps the Tsar’s paranoid-mystical framing and the comedic subject assignment (“the demons” as agents). No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. The mock-biblical proclamation is preserved, maintaining the joke’s religious certainty vs. mundane technology. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Captures the MC/announcer vibe and the mass-invitation energy that drives the gag. No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Preserves the breezy self-satisfaction and the criminal’s casual charm. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preventing confusion and preserving the Soviet-satire inversion. No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Keeps the comic inventory rhythm, the self-important “domestic,” and the trailing “Three” that signals exaggeration. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Perfect match to the approved/reference translation. Preserves the calm, sing-song fatalism and the shared-madness punchline. No issues.
